title: Platform Thinking
summary: Internal developer platforms, golden paths, platform as a product, reducing cognitive load, and team topologies.

sections.content:
## What is Platform Engineering?

Platform engineering builds **Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs)** — self-service tools that let application teams deploy, operate, and monitor services without deep infrastructure knowledge.

The goal: **reduce cognitive load** on development teams so they can focus on business logic, not infrastructure plumbing.

sections.title: Internal Developer Platform
sections.content:
graph TB
    DevTeam[Development Teams] -->|self-service| IDP[Internal Developer Platform]
    IDP --> CI[CI/CD Pipelines\n GitHub Actions templates]
    IDP --> K8s[Kubernetes Deployment\nHelm charts, operators]
    IDP --> Obs[Observability\nPre-configured dashboards]
    IDP --> Sec[Security\nOPA policies, secret injection]
    IDP --> DB[Database Provisioning\nSelf-service DB creation]
    IDP --> Docs[Service Catalog\nBackstage]
    style IDP fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style DevTeam fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Golden Paths

A **golden path** is the opinionated, supported way to build and deploy services at your organization. It encodes best practices into templates and tooling so developers "fall into the pit of success."

```
Golden Path for a new Spring Boot microservice:
1. Use project template: github.com/org/service-template
   → Pre-configured: actuator, security, observability, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions
2. Register in service catalog (Backstage)
3. Provision infra via GitOps (raise PR to infra repo)
4. Deploy: git push main → auto-deployed to staging → manual prod

Developer from idea to first deployment in < 1 hour
With all best practices (security, observability) included by default
```

### Why golden paths, not guardrails?
Guardrails say "you can't do X." Golden paths say "here's the easy right way." Teams can deviate, but the default is correct.

## Team Topologies

Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais (2019) defined four fundamental team types:

| Team Type | Purpose | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Stream-aligned** | Delivers a product/service end-to-end | Order Service team |
| **Platform** | Builds the internal platform | Platform Engineering team |
| **Enabling** | Helps other teams adopt practices | DevOps enablement team |
| **Complicated-subsystem** | Owns a complex component | ML platform team |

### Interaction Modes:
- **Collaboration** — work closely together for a period (temporarily)
- **X-as-a-Service** — platform team provides self-service capability
- **Facilitating** — enabling team coaches stream-aligned team

sections.title: Team Topologies Interaction
sections.content:
graph LR
    Platform[Platform Team] -->|X-as-a-Service| SA1[Stream Team: Orders]
    Platform -->|X-as-a-Service| SA2[Stream Team: Payments]
    Platform -->|X-as-a-Service| SA3[Stream Team: Users]
    Enable[Enabling Team\nDevOps/Security] -->|facilitating| SA1
    Enable -->|facilitating| SA2
    CS[Complex Subsystem\nML Team] -->|X-as-a-Service| SA1
    style Platform fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Enable fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style CS fill:#7c3aed,color:#fff

## Backstage — Service Catalog

Backstage (Spotify, now CNCF) is an open-source platform for building internal developer portals:

```yaml
# catalog-info.yaml in every service repo
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: order-service
  description: Manages order lifecycle
  annotations:
    github.com/project-slug: org/order-service
    backstage.io/techdocs-ref: dir:.
    pagerduty.com/service-id: P123ABC
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-orders
  system: ecommerce
  dependsOn:
    - component:payment-service
    - resource:orders-postgresql
  providesApis:
    - order-api
```

Backstage provides:
- Service catalog with ownership
- API documentation
- TechDocs (markdown docs)
- Software templates (create new service in UI)
- Plugins: CI/CD status, on-call, cost, security

rows.Characteristics: Each team manages own infra, no standards
Wiki pages, bash scripts, Confluence guides
Dedicated platform team, SLOs for platform

sections.title: Treat your platform as a product
sections.content: Platform engineering teams should treat internal developers as customers. Measure developer experience (DORA metrics, onboarding time, deployment frequency). Run internal NPS surveys. Prioritize self-service over 'raise a ticket.' The platform succeeds when developers choose to use it because it makes their lives easier, not because they're forced to.

sections.question: What is the primary difference between a 'golden path' and a 'guardrail' in platform engineering?
options: Golden paths are mandatory; guardrails are optional
Golden paths provide the easy, correct way to do things; guardrails enforce restrictions. Golden paths work with developers, guardrails work against them.
Guardrails are for security; golden paths are for performance
They are different names for the same concept
sections.explanation: Guardrails say 'you cannot do X' and require enforcement mechanisms. Golden paths say 'here is the easiest, supported way to do X correctly.' Developers can still deviate from golden paths (they're opinionated, not mandatory), but the default path bakes in best practices. Teams adopt golden paths because they reduce friction, not because they're forced to.
